behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily cross-module state handoff (git-history analyzer + age-threshold logic + PR-state input). Unit tests on each rule can still miss wiring defects. I considered System, but the behavior text does not require live/external infrastructure or explicit external contract validation; it only states detection criteria. No AI dependency, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Construct an in-process integration test that wires real detection modules together over a fixture repo graph and PR-state fixtures, then assert stale/non-stale outcomes for threshold and no-PR combinations.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in bootstrap orchestration: session-start path invoking staleness computation and formatting/emitting warning payload. Unit-only tests on formatter or detector can miss this interaction. I considered System, but behavior text does not require real shell/session process contracts; only that setup emits the warning with context.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start setup in a composed test harness with stale branch state injected through real internal modules, and assert emitted warning content includes branch name/age/actionable context.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly a local deterministic function mapping with no I/O, no module handoff requirement, no real infra, and no AI dependency. Higher-level checks add little additional reality signal for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit tests over branch-state inputs asserting exact label outputs and deterministic repeatability.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires operation against live repo and live PR state and validation in rendered output, which is a real boundary round-trip check. This hits REAL-INFRA and SYSTEM TRIAD (real external happy-path contract and visible output side effect). No AI model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in an environment connected to a real repo + real PR source state, then verify rendered output text/labels for both clean and stale-no-PR scenarios.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: This is rule interaction between branch-state computation and PR-status interpretation (open/merged exemptions). Unit tests per rule can miss combined logic. I considered System, but the behavior text does not require live PR API contract or real-network verification; it states classification correctness only.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test combining branch-state calculator and PR-status resolver with fixtures for open/merged/no-PR cases; assert only no-PR branches can become stale-no-pr.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real VCS history transformations (rebase/squash semantics) and round-trip observation after history rewrite; faked commit IDs can hide real git behavior differences. This requires exercising real subprocess/OS-backed git behavior and verifying post-operation classification.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real test repo, perform actual rebase and squash-merge flows that rewrite commit hashes, then run staleness detection and assert branch is no longer classified stale-no-pr when work is effectively integrated.
